preview_text:
The model rates Croatia as a strong favorite with a 79% win probability, leaving Ghana with only a 3% chance of victory. The gap between the two outcomes is substantial under the model's framework, reflecting a pronounced asymmetry in the forecast.

The model's distribution—79% win, 19% draw, 3% loss—suggests a match tilted decisively in Croatia's direction, though the 19% draw probability indicates the outcome remains genuinely uncertain rather than predetermined. A draw is the only plausible alternative to a Croatia win in this assessment; a Ghana victory rates as a tail-end scenario.

The model and market diverge significantly. The sportsbook consensus places Croatia at 58%, roughly 21 percentage points below the model, while assigning Ghana 17%—more than six times the model's 3% figure. Polymarket pricing aligns closely with the sportsbooks on Ghana and the draw. The dashboard does not adjudicate which view is correct; the divergence may reflect different information, methodology, or risk preferences in pricing.
